libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: For nativeTest/native-image execution of io.netty:netty-transport-classes-io_uring:4.2.4.Final, set runtime system properties `-Dsun.misc.unsafe.memory.access=allow` and `-Dio.netty.tryReflectionSetAccessible=true`. The existing 4.2.3 test scaffold already does this only for `4.2.3.Final`; extend that condition or make it apply to the tested 4.2.x version. Do not require the native io_uring classifier just to get coverage: the target artifact is the Java-side `classes` artifact, and tests can exercise the availability/unavailability contract when the native library is absent.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8594 [Automation] native-image run fails for io.netty:netty-transport-classes-io_uring:4.2.4.Final; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Full io_uring transport round-trip tests require the platform classifier artifact such as `io.netty:netty-transport-native-io_uring:<version>:linux-x86_64`, a supported Linux kernel, and sufficient memlock limits; adding that would change test assumptions and may make CI environment-dependent.
If the system properties are only applied to JVM tests and not nativeTest runtime arguments, local Gradle verification may pass while native-image run still fails.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra Maven dependency or Docker image is required for the classes artifact, but Java 25 native-image runs need Netty unsafe/reflection system properties before the existing meaningful tests can run.
